title: apply_member_patch returns Err(json_error) rather than setting a mutable update_err
description: Extracting member-patch logic into apply_member_patch -> Result<(), Value> is the right call. It allowed adding invalidPatch for unknown paths (the fix) and invalidArguments for bad roles, without nesting that logic inside the for loop. The Err(Value) return type is unusual but correct here — the JMAP error object is already a serde_json::Value and wrapping it in a typed error would require an extra conversion with no benefit. A contributor might try to replace the return type with a typed enum. The direct Value approach is appropriate for a JMAP implementation where the error format is defined by the spec.
close_reason: Decision recorded during review; no code change required
